subject: Databases
title: Materialized Views
description: A quick win: understand Materialized Views.

hook.text: A dashboard query joins five tables and aggregates millions of rows. It takes 30 seconds every time.

buildup.text: A materialized view stores the query result as a physical table. Reads are instant — it's pre-computed.

discovery.text: REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW recalculates it. Run it on a schedule or after significant data changes.

twist.text: Between refreshes, the view shows stale data. The trade-off: speed vs freshness.

climax.text: CONCURRENTLY option in Postgres refreshes without locking reads. Users see old data during refresh, not errors.

punchline.text: Materialized views: pre-computed query results. Fast reads, periodic refresh.

quiz.question: What is the trade-off of using materialized views?
options: Reads are very fast but the data can be stale between refreshes
Materialized views slow down all write operations
